steps.title: Pinpoint the core of the conclusion
steps.content: The team claims that head shape (anatomical feature) affects the ability to distinguish speech from nonspeech—this is the specific ability being explained, not language recognition more broadly.

steps.title: Match findings to the core claim
steps.content:
Choice B shows that compared with shorter-headed dogs, longer-headed dogs have a greater difference in brain activity when hearing speech (Spanish or Hungarian) than when hearing nonspeech (the scrambled recording).

The other choices focus on language-to-language distinctions, confound variables, or omit head-shape comparisons.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
